questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead

questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone

Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down

Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

A series of curves ahead

Road is slippery when wet — slow down

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

A railroad crossing is ahead

Sharp right then left turns ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Road construction or detour ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?

Slow down and proceed with caution

questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to come to a complete stop and wait until the signal turns green and the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic signal mean?

Proceed without slowing down

questions.explanation: A flashing red light means you must come to a complete stop and proceed only when it is safe, just like a stop sign.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light indicate?

questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that the signal is changing to red and you should stop unless you are too close to stop safely.

questions.prompt: A diamond-shaped sign is used to convey what type of message?

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow line on your side of the centerline mean?

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic signal mean?

questions.prompt: A downward-pointing triangular sign means what?
choices: Yield the right of way

questions.prompt: When a traffic signal is completely dark due to a power outage, you should treat the intersection as if it has what?
choices: A four-way stop
A green light
No control at all
A yield sign for all directions
questions.explanation: A dark or non-working signal should be treated as an all-way stop, with each driver yielding in turn.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, two vehicles arrive at the same time. Who has the right of way?
choices: The vehicle on the right
The vehicle on the left
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach an all-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When making a left turn at an intersection, you must yield to whom?
choices: Oncoming traffic and pedestrians
Only vehicles behind you
No one if your light is green
Only large trucks
questions.explanation: A driver turning left must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians in the crosswalk before completing the turn.

questions.prompt: In Illinois, when may you turn right on a red light?
choices: After a complete stop, unless a sign prohibits it
Never under any condition
Only between 6 a.m. and 6 p.m.
Without stopping if no police are present
questions.explanation: Illinois allows a right turn on red after you stop completely and yield, unless a posted sign forbids it.

questions.prompt: What should you do when an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on?
choices: Pull to the right edge of the road and stop
Speed up to get out of the area
Stop immediately in your lane
Turn left to clear the road
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right and stopping until they have passed.

questions.prompt: Who has the right of way at a marked crosswalk?
choices: Pedestrians in the crosswalk
Turning vehicles
Vehicles going straight
The first vehicle to honk
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians lawfully within a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When entering a roadway from a private driveway, you should do what?
choices: Yield to all traffic and pedestrians already on the road
Proceed because you have the right of way
Honk and merge quickly
Stop only for large vehicles
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering from a driveway must yield to traffic and pedestrians already using the roadway.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection where you and another car arrive about the same time, you should do what?
choices: Yield to the vehicle on your right
Always go first
Yield to the vehicle on your left
Both stop and wait indefinitely
questions.explanation: At an intersection with no signs or signals, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: Before merging onto a highway from an on-ramp, you should do what?
choices: Match the speed of highway traffic and yield to vehicles already on the highway
Stop at the end of the ramp and wait
Enter slowly and force traffic to slow
Cross all lanes immediately
questions.explanation: You should accelerate to match traffic speed and yield to vehicles already on the highway before merging.

questions.prompt: When two roads meet and one is a through highway with a stop sign on the smaller road, who yields?
choices: The driver facing the stop sign
The driver on the through highway
Whoever is going faster
Neither driver
questions.explanation: The driver controlled by the stop sign must stop and yield to traffic on the through highway.

questions.prompt: When you want to make a U-turn, you should first do what?
choices: Make sure it is legal and that you have a clear view of traffic in both directions
Turn quickly without signaling
Assume oncoming traffic will stop
Use the shoulder to complete the turn
questions.explanation: A U-turn should only be made where legal and where you can see clearly in both directions to yield to traffic.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit in an Illinois urban district?
choices: 30 mph
45 mph
55 mph
20 mph
questions.explanation: Illinois sets a default urban district speed limit of 30 mph unless another limit is posted.

questions.prompt: What does the 'basic speed law' require?
choices: Driving at a speed that is reasonable and safe for current conditions
Always driving exactly at the posted limit
Driving as fast as the car can safely go
Driving 10 mph below the limit at all times
questions.explanation: The basic speed law requires you to adjust your speed to be safe for the conditions, which may be below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: What is a good rule for keeping a safe following distance in good conditions?
choices: The three-second rule
Stay one car length back
Follow as close as possible to save fuel
Keep ten feet back at any speed
questions.explanation: The three-second rule lets you keep enough space to stop safely; increase it in poor conditions.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the default speed limit on most rural two-lane Illinois highways is what?
choices: 55 mph
70 mph
40 mph
65 mph
questions.explanation: The default limit on most rural two-lane highways in Illinois is 55 mph unless a different limit is posted.

questions.prompt: When should you increase your following distance beyond three seconds?
choices: In rain, snow, fog, or at night
Only on dry, sunny days
When traffic is light
Never, three seconds is always enough
questions.explanation: Poor weather and reduced visibility increase stopping distance, so allow extra following space.

questions.prompt: Posted speed limits represent what?
choices: The maximum speed allowed under ideal conditions
The minimum speed you must drive
A suggested speed only
The speed to drive in all weather
questions.explanation: A posted limit is the maximum legal speed for good conditions; you must slow down when conditions worsen.

questions.prompt: When driving through a work zone with workers present, you should do what?
choices: Slow down and obey the reduced work-zone speed limit
Maintain your normal speed
Speed up to leave the zone quickly
Change lanes repeatedly
questions.explanation: Work zones have lower posted limits and increased penalties to protect workers, so reduce your speed.

questions.prompt: If you are being tailgated by another driver, what is the safest response?
choices: Increase your following distance from the car ahead and let the tailgater pass
Brake suddenly to warn them
Speed up well above the limit
Block them from passing
questions.explanation: Adding space ahead gives you more room to stop gradually, and allowing the tailgater to pass reduces risk.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it can do what?
choices: Cause other drivers to brake suddenly or make risky passes
Always improve safety
Reduce fuel use to zero
Eliminate the need to signal
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly disrupts traffic flow and can lead to dangerous passing and rear-end situations.

questions.prompt: What is the default speed limit on many Illinois rural interstate highways unless otherwise posted?
choices: 65 mph
30 mph
45 mph
75 mph
questions.explanation: Rural interstates in Illinois generally carry a 65 mph limit, though some highways and tollways are posted up to 70 mph.

questions.prompt: When is passing prohibited?
choices: When a solid yellow line is on your side of the road
Whenever you are in a hurry
On any multilane highway
When the road is straight
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means it is unsafe and illegal to pass there.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, the left lane is generally intended for what?
choices: Passing slower vehicles
Parking
Slow-moving traffic
Emergency vehicles only
questions.explanation: Keep right except to pass; the left lane is meant for passing, not for cruising slowly.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should always do what?
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and check your blind spot
Just use your mirrors
Speed up sharply
Honk your horn
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking mirrors plus your blind spot ensures the lane is clear before you move over.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, when is it safe to return to your lane?
choices: When you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
As soon as you are alongside it
Immediately after you signal
Only after the next intersection
questions.explanation: Seeing the whole vehicle in your mirror means you have enough room to merge back safely.

questions.prompt: You should not attempt to pass another vehicle when you are within how far of an intersection or railroad crossing?
choices: 100 feet
10 feet
500 feet
1 mile
questions.explanation: Passing within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing is unsafe and prohibited.

questions.prompt: When may you legally pass on the right?
choices: When a vehicle ahead is turning left and there is room on a road with two or more lanes in your direction
Anytime you want to go faster
On the shoulder of any road
Never, passing on the right is always illegal
questions.explanation: Passing on the right is allowed only in limited situations, such as when a vehicle is turning left and a usable lane exists.

questions.prompt: A broken white line between lanes means what?
choices: You may change lanes when it is safe
You may never cross it
The road is one-way
Passing is prohibited
questions.explanation: Broken white lines separate same-direction lanes and allow lane changes when safe.

questions.prompt: You should not pass another vehicle when approaching what?
choices: A hill or curve where you cannot see oncoming traffic
A long, straight, flat road
A green traffic light
An empty parking lot
questions.explanation: Passing requires a clear view of oncoming traffic, which you do not have near hills or curves.

questions.prompt: When a school bus has its red lights flashing and stop arm extended on a two-way road, you must do what?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing
Slow to 15 mph and pass
Pass carefully on the left
Honk and continue
questions.explanation: You must stop for a school bus loading or unloading children and wait until the red lights stop flashing.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of a center two-way left-turn lane (marked with yellow lines)?
choices: For left turns by traffic moving in either direction
For passing slow vehicles
For parking during the day
For high-speed through traffic
questions.explanation: A center two-way left-turn lane is shared by both directions only for making left turns, not for through travel.

questions.prompt: In Illinois, when passing a bicyclist you must leave at least how much space?
choices: 3 feet
1 foot
6 inches
10 feet
questions.explanation: Illinois law requires drivers to give bicyclists at least 3 feet of clearance when passing.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have large blind spots, which means what for nearby drivers?
choices: If you cannot see the driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you
Trucks can always see all around them
You can safely tailgate trucks
Trucks stop faster than cars
questions.explanation: Trucks have large blind spots, so staying where you can see the mirrors keeps you visible to the driver.

questions.prompt: Illinois Scott's Law (the Move Over Law) requires drivers approaching a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights to do what?
choices: Slow down and change lanes away from it when possible
Speed up to clear the area
Stop in the lane next to it
Ignore it if traffic is heavy
questions.explanation: Scott's Law requires you to slow down and, when safe, move over to give room to stopped emergency and service vehicles.

questions.prompt: Why are motorcycles easy to overlook in traffic?
choices: They are smaller and can be hidden in blind spots
They are always brightly lit
They are larger than cars
They never travel on highways
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are small and easily hidden, so check carefully before turning or changing lanes.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog, you should do what?
choices: Yield the right of way and stop if necessary
Honk to alert them
Proceed quickly past them
Expect them to yield to you
questions.explanation: Pedestrians who are blind have the right of way, and drivers must yield and stop when needed.

questions.prompt: When following a motorcycle, you should do what?
choices: Allow a greater following distance than for a car
Follow more closely than a car
Use your high beams
Pass it immediately
questions.explanation: Motorcycles can stop quickly and are less stable, so give extra following distance.

questions.prompt: When you approach slow-moving farm equipment on a rural road, you should do what?
choices: Slow down and pass only when it is safe and legal
Pass immediately regardless of conditions
Tailgate to encourage it to move
Honk continuously
questions.explanation: Farm equipment moves slowly, so reduce speed and pass only when you have a clear, legal opportunity.

questions.prompt: Large trucks making a right turn may do what?
choices: Swing wide to the left before turning right
Always turn from the far right edge
Turn faster than cars
Stop in the middle of the intersection
questions.explanation: Trucks often need extra room and swing left before a right turn, so do not try to squeeze between the truck and the curb.

questions.prompt: When a pedestrian is crossing at an intersection without a marked crosswalk, you should do what?
choices: Yield to the pedestrian
Continue because there is no crosswalk
Speed up to pass before they cross
Honk and proceed
questions.explanation: Pedestrians have the right of way at intersections even where crosswalks are not painted.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow light on a slow-moving vehicle or an orange triangle emblem means what?
choices: The vehicle travels well below normal traffic speed
The vehicle is an emergency vehicle
You must stop completely
The vehicle is parked
questions.explanation: The orange slow-moving-vehicle emblem warns that the vehicle moves much slower than regular traffic.

questions.prompt: For drivers age 21 and over in Illinois, it is illegal to drive with a BAC at or above what level?
choices: 0.08 percent
0.10 percent
0.05 percent
0.02 percent
questions.explanation: A BAC of 0.08 percent or higher is the legal limit for DUI for drivers 21 and older.

questions.prompt: Under Illinois' Zero Tolerance Law, a driver under age 21 may have what BAC?
choices: No measurable alcohol (above 0.00 results in suspension)
Up to 0.08 percent
Up to 0.05 percent
Up to 0.02 percent
questions.explanation: Illinois has zero tolerance for drivers under 21, so any trace of alcohol leads to license suspension.

questions.prompt: In Illinois, using a handheld cell phone to talk or text while driving is what?
choices: Illegal; drivers must use hands-free operation
Allowed at any time
Allowed only on highways
Allowed while stopped at red lights
questions.explanation: Illinois requires hands-free use, so holding a phone to talk, text, or browse while driving is prohibited.

questions.prompt: What is the only thing that will reduce a person's blood alcohol concentration?
choices: Time
Coffee
A cold shower
Exercise
questions.explanation: Only time allows the body to remove alcohol; coffee and other tricks do not speed it up.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect driving ability?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment and coordination
It improves concentration
It sharpens reflexes
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Alcohol impairs the skills needed to drive safely, even at low levels.

questions.prompt: Texting while driving is dangerous mainly because it does what?
choices: Takes your eyes, hands, and attention off driving
Improves your awareness
Has no effect at low speeds
Is only risky on highways
questions.explanation: Texting combines visual, manual, and mental distraction, greatly increasing crash risk.

questions.prompt: Besides alcohol, what else can impair your ability to drive safely?
choices: Illegal drugs and some prescription or over-the-counter medications
Only illegal drugs
Nothing if you feel fine
Only very large doses of alcohol
questions.explanation: Many legal and illegal drugs can impair driving, so check labels and avoid driving while affected.

questions.prompt: If you plan to drink alcohol, the safest choice is to do what?
choices: Arrange a sober driver or another way home in advance
Drink only beer
Wait 30 minutes before driving
Eat a large meal and drive
questions.explanation: Planning a sober ride before drinking is the only reliable way to avoid impaired driving.

questions.prompt: A first Zero Tolerance Law offense for a driver under 21 in Illinois results in what?
choices: A 3-month suspension of driving privileges
A warning only
A 1-year suspension
No penalty
questions.explanation: A first violation of the Zero Tolerance Law suspends a young driver's privileges for 3 months.

questions.prompt: What is one effective way to avoid distraction while driving?
choices: Set up navigation and music before you start driving
Read messages at stoplights
Eat meals while driving
Hold your phone in your lap
questions.explanation: Handling distractions before you drive keeps your attention on the road once you are moving.

questions.prompt: Illinois law requires you to turn on your headlights whenever you are using what?
choices: Your windshield wipers because of weather
Your radio
Your turn signals
Your air conditioning
questions.explanation: Illinois requires headlights to be on any time weather conditions require you to use your wipers.

questions.prompt: If your car begins to skid, what should you do?
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Brake hard and hold the wheel still
Accelerate to regain control
Close your eyes and brace
questions.explanation: Easing off the gas and steering gently toward your intended path helps you regain control of a skid.

questions.prompt: In foggy conditions, you should use which headlights?
choices: Low beams
High beams
No lights
Hazard lights while moving normally
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so use low beams in fog.

questions.prompt: Why is the road often most slippery during the first rain after a dry spell?
choices: Oil and dust on the road rise to the surface
Water makes tires grip better
The road is coldest then
Rain removes all traction permanently
questions.explanation: The first rain lifts oil and grime to the surface, making the road especially slick before it washes away.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning, where tires lose contact with the road on water, is best prevented by doing what?
choices: Reducing your speed on wet roads
Increasing your speed
Braking hard
Turning off your headlights
questions.explanation: Lower speeds keep your tires in contact with the pavement and reduce the chance of hydroplaning.

questions.prompt: At night, you should dim your high beams within how many feet of an oncoming vehicle?
choices: 500 feet
100 feet
50 feet
1,000 feet
questions.explanation: Dim high beams within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle to avoid blinding the other driver.

questions.prompt: When driving at night, you should adjust your speed so that you can do what?
choices: Stop within the distance your headlights light up
Drive faster than in daytime
Match the posted limit regardless of visibility
Rely on other cars' lights
questions.explanation: Overdriving your headlights means you cannot stop in time for hazards, so slow down at night.

questions.prompt: On icy roads, bridges and overpasses are especially dangerous because they do what?
choices: Freeze before other parts of the road
Stay warmer than the road
Always have more traction
Are never slippery
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses lose heat from both sides and ice over sooner than the surrounding road.

questions.prompt: If you are caught in heavy rain and visibility is very poor, you should do what?
choices: Slow down, turn on low-beam headlights, and increase following distance
Speed up to get out of the rain
Turn on high beams
Stop suddenly in the travel lane
questions.explanation: Reducing speed, using low beams, and adding space help you stay in control and visible in heavy rain.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle stalls on railroad tracks and a train is approaching, you should do what?
choices: Get out and move away from the tracks immediately
Stay in the car and try to restart it
Push the car forward
Wait for the train to stop
questions.explanation: Your life matters more than the vehicle, so exit and move well away from the tracks at an angle toward the train.

questions.prompt: How close to a fire hydrant are you prohibited from parking?
choices: Within 15 feet
Within 5 feet
Within 50 feet
Within 100 feet
questions.explanation: You may not park within 15 feet of a fire hydrant so firefighters can reach it.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill, you should turn your front wheels which way?
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning wheels toward the curb when facing downhill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: In Illinois, seat belts are required for the driver and all passengers of what age?
choices: Age 8 and older
Age 16 and older
Age 18 and older
Front-seat occupants only
questions.explanation: Illinois law requires everyone age 8 and older in any seat to wear a seat belt.

questions.prompt: In Illinois, children under what age must be secured in an appropriate child restraint system?
choices: Age 8
Age 4
Age 12
Age 6
questions.explanation: Children under age 8 must ride in an appropriate child safety seat or restraint.

questions.prompt: When parking uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels which way?
choices: Away from the curb
Toward the curb
Straight ahead
Sharply left
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn wheels away from the curb so the car rolls back into it if it moves.

questions.prompt: Where are you not allowed to park?
choices: In a crosswalk or on a sidewalk
On a residential street at night
In a marked parking space
In a driveway you own
questions.explanation: Parking in a crosswalk or on a sidewalk blocks pedestrians and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: Before pulling away from a parallel parking space at the curb, you should do what?
choices: Signal and yield to passing traffic
Pull out quickly without signaling
Assume traffic will stop
Back up first
questions.explanation: You must signal and yield to traffic already in the lane before leaving a curbside space.

questions.prompt: You may park in a space reserved for persons with disabilities only if you have what?
choices: A valid disability placard or license plate
An emergency
Permission from a passenger
Your hazard lights on
questions.explanation: Disability parking spaces require a valid placard or special plate; parking there otherwise is illegal.

questions.prompt: After parking on a hill, in addition to turning your wheels you should do what?
choices: Set the parking brake and leave the car in park or in gear
Leave the car in neutral
Leave the engine running
Leave the doors unlocked
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake and leaving the car in park or gear helps keep it from rolling.

questions.prompt: When parking on a hill with no curb, you should turn your front wheels which way?
choices: Toward the edge of the road
Straight ahead
Toward the center of the road
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Without a curb, turn the wheels toward the road's edge so a rolling car heads off the road, not into traffic.

questions.prompt: In Illinois, at what age may a teen apply for an instruction permit when enrolled in driver education?
questions.explanation: A teen enrolled in an approved driver education course may apply for an instruction permit at age 15.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised practice driving must an Illinois permit holder complete?
choices: 50 hours, including 10 at night
20 hours, including 5 at night
100 hours total
10 hours total
questions.explanation: Illinois requires 50 hours of supervised practice, with at least 10 of those hours at night.

questions.prompt: What minimum score is required to pass the Illinois knowledge exam?
choices: 80 percent
70 percent
90 percent
60 percent
questions.explanation: You must score at least 80 percent to pass the Illinois written knowledge test.

questions.prompt: While a permit holder is driving in Illinois, who must be in the front passenger seat?
choices: A licensed driver at least 21 years old
Any licensed driver
A friend with a permit
No one is required
questions.explanation: A supervising driver age 21 or older with a valid license must ride in the front seat with a permit holder.

questions.prompt: How long must an Illinois teen hold an instruction permit before getting a license?
choices: At least 9 months
At least 1 month
At least 6 months
At least 2 years
questions.explanation: Illinois requires teens to hold the instruction permit for at least 9 months in the GDL program.

questions.prompt: The Illinois written knowledge test includes questions on which two main areas?
choices: Road signs and traffic laws
History and geography
Vehicle repair and finance
Insurance rates and taxes
questions.explanation: The exam tests your knowledge of road signs and Illinois traffic laws and safe driving practices.

questions.prompt: If you change your address, you are generally required to do what?
choices: Notify the Secretary of State within the required time
Do nothing
Wait until your license expires
Get a new license plate only
questions.explanation: Drivers must keep their address current with the Secretary of State so records and notices reach them.

questions.prompt: A teen under 18 applying for a permit in Illinois generally needs what?
choices: Parent or guardian consent and proof of school enrollment
Only a birth certificate
A passing road test only
Nothing additional
questions.explanation: Applicants under 18 need parental consent and must show proof of school enrollment or graduation.

questions.prompt: During the initial licensing period, a newly licensed Illinois driver under 18 faces what restriction?
choices: Limits on the number of passengers and a nighttime driving curfew
No restrictions at all
A ban on highway driving
A requirement to drive only on weekends
questions.explanation: New teen drivers face passenger limits and a nighttime curfew during the first part of the GDL licensing phase.

questions.prompt: Under Illinois' implied consent law, refusing a lawful chemical test after a DUI arrest results in what?
choices: An automatic statutory summary suspension of driving privileges
A warning only
No consequence
A small fine only
questions.explanation: By driving in Illinois you agree to chemical testing, and refusing one leads to an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: What can happen if you accumulate too many moving violations on your Illinois driving record?
choices: Your license can be suspended or revoked
Nothing changes
You get a cash reward
Your insurance is cancelled by the state
questions.explanation: Too many violations add points and can lead to suspension or revocation of your driving privileges.

questions.prompt: Violating Illinois' Scott's Law (Move Over Law) can result in what?
choices: Fines and other penalties
A verbal warning only
No penalty
A free safety class
questions.explanation: Failing to slow down and move over for emergency vehicles carries fines and can bring steeper penalties for serious cases.

questions.prompt: A DUI conviction in Illinois can lead to which of the following?
choices: Fines, possible jail time, and loss of your license
Only a warning
A reduced insurance rate
Automatic license renewal
questions.explanation: DUI is a serious offense with penalties including fines, possible jail, and loss of driving privileges.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash that causes injury or significant property damage, you are required to do what?
choices: Stop, provide information, and render aid as required by law
Leave the scene if no police are present
Continue driving and report it next week
Exchange only phone numbers and leave
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of a crash is illegal; you must stop, exchange information, and help the injured.

questions.prompt: What is the consequence of violating the hands-free cell phone law in Illinois?
choices: It is a moving violation that can bring fines and points
It is never enforced
It only applies to commercial drivers
It carries no penalty for a first offense
questions.explanation: Handheld phone use is a moving violation in Illinois and can add fines and points to your record.

questions.prompt: Why are penalties stricter for drivers under 21 who break alcohol-related driving laws?
choices: Illinois enforces a zero-tolerance policy for underage drinking and driving
Young drivers pay lower fines
There is no difference by age
Penalties are only for drivers over 21
questions.explanation: Because underage drinking is illegal, any alcohol while driving under 21 triggers strict zero-tolerance penalties.

questions.prompt: A police officer signals you to pull over. You should do what?
choices: Signal, pull over safely to the right, and stop
Speed up to find a better spot
Stop in the middle of the lane
Ignore the officer if you did nothing wrong
questions.explanation: When signaled by police, pull over safely to the right as soon as you can and stop.

questions.prompt: For a teen driver, violating GDL passenger or curfew rules can result in what?
choices: Delayed progress in the licensing program and extended restrictions
Immediate full license
A cash reward
No effect at all
questions.explanation: Breaking GDL rules can set back a young driver's progress and keep restrictions in place longer.

questions.prompt: Driving without a valid license or while suspended in Illinois can result in what?
choices: Fines and possible additional penalties
A simple warning every time
No legal consequences
An automatic license upgrade
questions.explanation: Driving without a valid license or while suspended is an offense that brings fines and further penalties.
